history.strategy_update: Implement conflict-aware period assignment using graph coloring: (1) Build a conflict graph from student enrollment data. (2) Identify all exam cliques (fully-connected groups). (3) Assign conflicting exams to different periods: 3-cliques need ≥3 periods, 2-conflicts need ≥2 periods. (4) Distribute non-conflicting exams to fill remaining capacity. (5) Verify no two exams in any period share students before finalizing.

history.strategy_update: Construct a conflict graph by analyzing exam_students data to identify which exam pairs share students. Before assigning any exam to a period, check whether ANY other exam already assigned to that period shares students with it. Use greedy assignment: sort exams by conflict degree (most constrained first), and for each exam, try available periods in order, assigning to the first period that has no student conflicts with already-scheduled exams. This respects hard constraints: room capacity, period/room availability, and distribution constraints (same-period C1, precedence C2, different-period C3).
